sound_type: raspy caterwauling yowl -- 'RRAAAAOWWW'. 400-1,500 Hz. 1-3 seconds. Territorial/mating. E=5
ecosystem: Forest and brushland. Mixed forest, chaparral, suburban edges, rocky outcrops. Most adaptable felid.
season: Year-round. Yowl peaks in breeding season (January-March).

coordinates.description: Bobcat throughout PNW forests and brushland, including suburban edges.

taxonomy_note.description: Third Felidae. Felidae quartet (212-215). The smaller, more adaptable PNW felid.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Felidae: Cougar Scream (212, E=7), Cougar Chirp (213, E=4), Bobcat Yowl (214, E=5).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Lynx rufus = 'reddish lynx'. rufus from Latin 'red/reddish'. Bobcat from 'bobbed' tail.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Lynx -- 4 species worldwide. Bobcat and Canada Lynx both in PNW.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Felidae: third entry. Smaller cat. Generalist predator.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. 7-14 kg. Most adaptable felid in North America.

song_metrics.note_types: Territorial yowl (raspy, drawn-out), hiss (defensive), growl (warning), bark (alarm), purr (contentment).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Sequences during mating season. Multiple bobcats may yowl in chorus.
song_metrics.timbre: Raspy, scratchy, aggressive. Like a large angry house cat.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Mid-range predator. Between cougar scream (E=7) and chirp (E=4).
song_metrics.tempo: Yowls at irregular intervals during mating bouts.
song_metrics.context: Breeding season (Jan-Mar). Territorial disputes. Male advertisement.
song_metrics.key_property: BOBCAT YOWL. Raspy caterwauling. E=5. Third Felidae. v1.49.355.

pelage.overall: Tawny-brown with dark spots/streaks. Short 'bobbed' tail with dark tip. Ear tufts (small).
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Short bobbed tail (10-18 cm). Spotted/streaked coat. Ear tufts. Smaller than cougar.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Slight seasonal variation. Coat slightly thicker and grayer in winter.

physical_description.overall: 7-14 kg (15-30 lbs). Body length 65-105 cm. Short tail 10-18 cm. Muscular, compact.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Short bobbed tail. Spotted coat. Ear tufts. Compact and muscular.
physical_description.field_id: Small tawny cat with short tail and spots. Much smaller than cougar. Often glimpsed at dawn/dusk near suburban edges. Reclusive but not as cryptic as cougar.

nodes.title: Raspy Yowl -- Felid Vocal Template
nodes.description: Same basic vocalization template as house cat, diverged 10 million years ago. Scaled-up caterwauling. 400-1,500 Hz.

nodes.title: Habitat Generalism -- Most Adaptable Felid
nodes.description: Forest to suburban edge. 20,000-30,000 in WA. The most habitat-generalist felid in North America.

nodes.title: Cougar vs. Bobcat -- Two PNW Felids
nodes.description: 50-100 kg vs. 7-14 kg. Deep forest vs. everywhere. Same family, different niches and scales.

cross_references.relationship: FELIDAE SIZE COMPARISON! Cougar (E=7, 50-100 kg) vs. bobcat (E=5, 7-14 kg). Same family, different scale.
FELIDAE TRIO! Scream (E=7), chirp (E=4), yowl (E=5). Three voices in three degrees.
LYNX GENUS! Same genus. Yowl (E=5) to yowl+chatter (E=5). Different niches.
SMALLER PREDATOR FEROCITY! Power trio (E=6) and small cat (E=5). Both punch above weight.
